sections.paragraph: Num 22:1 also יְרִיחֹו Jos 2:1, Jos 2:2, Jos 2:3, and יְרִיחֹה 1Ki 16:34 Jericho, a well known city of Palestine, situated in the neighbourhood of the Jordan and the Dead Sea, in the territory of Benjamin, in a very fertile district; LXX. Ἱεριχώ, Strabo Ἱερικοῦς, xvi. 2, § 41, Arabic ريحا, ريحى Rîha, see Relandi Palæstina, p. 383, and 829, seq. (If the primary form be יְרֵחֹו, it may be rendered city of the moon, from יָרֵחַ and ֹו, הֹ=ֹון, as in the words עַכֹּו, שְׁלֹמֹה, שִׁילֹו; if יְרִיחֹו, it signifies a fragrant place, from the root רִיחַ.)
